fields.summary: Allow jobs to be submitted to reservation that is active but does not have any allocations

status.description: The issue is open and ready for the assignee to start work on it.
status.name: Open

issuetype.description: An improvement or enhancement to an existing feature or task.
issuetype.name: Improvement

fields.description: YARN-1051 introduces the notion of reserving resources prior to job submission. A reservation is active from its arrival time to deadline but in the interim there can be instances of time when it does not have any resources allocated. We reject jobs that are submitted when the reservation allocation is zero. Instead we should accept & queue the jobs till the resources become available.

resolution.description: A fix for this issue is checked into the tree and tested.
resolution.name: Fixed

comments.body: Attaching a simple patch that skips the overriding of max system apps for dynamic ReservationQueues based on their absolute capacity to allow accepting & queuing jobs against the reservation till resources are allocated.

| Console output | https://builds.apache.org/job/PreCommit-YARN-Build/8639/console |


This message was automatically generated.

Subru, this patch makes sense to me. The behavior you describe is the intended one, as we discussed.  

One nit: 
please extend the tests to validate that the maxApplications is and stays set correctly, so we defend against future evolution of the code that might miss this subtle case.
Thanks [~curino] for reviewing the patch. I am uploading a new patch that adds a check to the _maxApps_ in _ReservationQueue_ post invocations of _setEntitlement()_

\\

+1 on this. I committed this to trunk, branch-2, branch-2.7.1 as it was (basically) a bug fix, that was worth back-porting. Thanks [~subru] for the contribution and quick turnaround adding the tests.
Hi [~curino], would you commit it to branch-2.7 instead of branch-2.7.1? 2.7.1 was already released, so we should update the fix version and changelog.
